faq.question: What can I sign?
faq.answer: Sigstore tooling supports signing any artifacts, such as files or containers. Rekor supports many file formats, such as in-toto attestations, JARs, RPMs, or Alpine images.

faq.question: Is Sigstore easy to use?
faq.answer: Signing and verifying software benefits the entire ecosystem, so we have made our tools easy to adopt and use. You can see how easy it is by taking a look at our [Cosign quickstart guide](https://docs.sigstore.dev/quickstart/quickstart-cosign/) or our [CI quickstart guide](https://docs.sigstore.dev/quickstart/quickstart-ci/). The Sigstore project is also customizeable and complex, so implementing advanced features (like using your own instance of Rekor, for example) will take a bit longer. Detailed information on each Sigstore tool is available in our [developer documentation](https://docs.sigstore.dev/).

faq.question: How do I learn more about Sigstore?
faq.answer: Our [documentation](https://docs.sigstore.dev) includes extensive information about all the tools that make up Sigstore. It also includes quickstart guides, a discussion of our security model, and information about our language-specific clients and CI integrations. Other resources include the project's [YouTube channel](https://www.youtube.com/@projectsigstore), the [OpenSSF's website](https://openssf.org/), and the [Sigstore Blog](https://blog.sigstore.dev/).

faq.question: How do I get help if I have a question?
faq.answer: The fastest way to get answers, ideas and feedback is on [Sigstore's Slack](https://sigstore.slack.com/join/shared_invite/zt-2ub0ztl5z-PkWb_Ldwef5d6nb~oryaTA), or to post an issue on one of [Sigstore's repositories](https://github.com/sigstore).

faq.question: How can I get involved?
faq.answer: The best way is to get involved is to try out the technologies and let us know your thoughts! We welcome new members to the working group over on [Slack](https://sigstore.slack.com/join/shared_invite/zt-2ub0ztl5z-PkWb_Ldwef5d6nb~oryaTA) and you can join the mailing list at sigstore-dev@googlegroups.com. You are also welcome to join us on our [community calls](https://openssf.org/calendar/). They are typically on the third Tuesday of every month @ 16:30 UTC.

faq.question: What is Sigstore's current status?
faq.answer:
Sigstore became an OpenSSF graduated project in March 2024. Other important dates include:
 - Sigstore announced general availability (GA) for the Rekor transparency log and Fulcio certificate authority public benefit services in October 2022.
- Cosign v3 was made available in October 2025.
- Sigstore-signed in-toto attestations were adopted by Homebrew in May 2024, PyPI in November 2024, and Maven Central in January 2025.
- Model signing in NVIDIA’s NGC was adopted in July 2025.
- Language specific clients were released in 2024 and 2025.
 In short, Sigstore is actively growing both as a technology and as a community.
